items.title: AAIC 2026: Biogen delivers hope, validation with anti-tau Alzheimer’s win
items.snippet: Biogen touted an “unprecedented” drop in tau in a Phase 2 trial, backing the company’s decision to take diranersen to Phase 3 despite a missed primary endpoint and seemingly supporting the anti-tau approach.

items.title: Final psychedelics guidance suggests FDA is 'receptive' to helping companies succeed
items.snippet: The agency’s finalized recommendations for clinical testing of psychedelic compounds for mental health could pave the way for companies like Compass Pathways, whose psilocybin-based therapy for treatment-resistant depression could win approval as early as this year.

items.title: Bonus: Q2 2026 Job Market Update
items.snippet: In this bonus episode, BioSpace Managing Editor Jef Akst and Angela Gabriel, content manager, life sciences careers, look at the Q2 job market and discuss encouraging signs for job seekers.

items.title: Spero bets up to $1.1B for Innovent’s autoimmune antibody
items.snippet: Spero Therapeutics is putting over $1 billion on the line in exchange for rights to develop SP001, an anti-CD40L antibody, for IgG4-related disease. A Phase 2 study is planned for the second quarter of 2027.

items.title: AstraZeneca takes another China trip, picking Dizal’s lung cancer drug for up to $1.5B
items.snippet: Dizal Pharmaceutical’s Zegfrovy is approved in the U.S. for locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer. For $600 million upfront, AstraZeneca will gain global rights to advance and commercialize the asset.

items.title: FDA proposes streamlined requirements for ‘hub-and-spoke’ manufacturing models
items.snippet: Through the proposal, the FDA could clear barriers to distributed manufacturing approaches intended to enhance emergency preparedness and supply chain resilience.

items.title: Biogen, Eisai win FDA nod for starter dose of subcutaneous Leqembi
items.snippet: Leqembi Iqlik can now be given at home throughout the entire course of treatment, setting it apart from Eli Lilly’s Kisunla, the only other anti-amyloid Alzheimer’s disease therapy on the market.

items.title: Exit of Roche’s embattled Huntington’s drug ‘tightens’ case for Wave’s more selective ASO
items.snippet: The failure of Roche’s Ionis-partnered tominersen in Huntington’s disease may indicate that Wave Life Sciences’ allele-specific antisense oligonucleotide candidate WVE-003 is on the right track, according to analysts at Rodman & Renshaw.

items.title: Report: Biopharma Job Market Report Q2 2026
items.snippet: Gain the latest insights into the biopharma hiring landscape with BioSpace's Biopharma Job Market Report Q2 2026. Explore the trends shaping talent acquisition, from rising job postings and sustained R&D hiring to the market activity signaling stronger hiring demand ahead.

items.title: EMD Serono may trim R&D workforce by up to 70
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: BIO 2026: As ADCs evolve, experts say ‘wellness’ needs to be factored into patient survival
items.snippet: As antibody-drug conjugates advance and move into earlier lines of treatment, drug developers have to build gentler therapies that don’t just extend survival but improve it.

items.title: AB Science ends 3 studies in multiple sclerosis, other immune conditions
items.snippet: The trials, which were testing the tyrosine kinase inhibitor masitinib, had previously been paused. AB Science’s decision not to resume them was a matter of prioritization, not safety, the biotech said.

items.title: FDA posts new rejection letters days after confirming pause
items.snippet: The FDA last week confirmed that it would suspend the release of complete response letters pending the resolution of a citizen petition from an unnamed pharma company. Nevertheless, 14 new CRLs are now available on the agency’s portal.

items.title: The FDA needs a patient-first vision—and adcomms with patients at the table
items.snippet: FDA’s rare disease decisions are strongest when the patient community has a voice in advisory committee decisions.

items.title: From sequence to scale: Gene editing’s new era in biologics manufacturing
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you’ll hear from Jack Crawford, CEO of Demeetra, and Magnus Gustavsson, chief commercial officer at NorthX Biologics. We unpack the evolution of cell line development — CHO cells, targeted integration, transposases and the collaboration models speeding biologics from sequence to GMP.

items.title: AAIC 2026: Tau-targeted Alzheimer’s treatments heat up while amyloid therapies persist
items.snippet: The lineup at the Alzheimer’s Association International Conference will provide critical insight into where the industry is headed with regard to targets being explored to vanquish the elusive neurodegenerative disease.

items.title: GSK and Hansoh’s ADC clinches late-stage win, teeing up regulatory submission in China
items.snippet: GSK and Hansoh Pharmaceutical’s antibody-drug conjugate success validates their partnership, one of the many deals in which Big Pharma has tapped a China company for promising cancer candidates.

items.title: AstraZeneca, Ionis’ Wainua woes send waves across ATTR-CM space
items.snippet: The failure of AstraZeneca and Ionis’ Wainua in a late-stage study of ATTR-CM casts doubt on Alnylam’s next-generation candidate but is good news for others in the space, including BridgeBio and Intellia Therapeutics.

items.title: Roche scraps 2 Huntington’s studies, handing Ionis more bad news
items.snippet: Roche’s decision to discontinue the Ionis-partnered trials came soon after the biotech sustained a late-stage failure in ATTR-CM.

items.title: Amgen continues to defend Tavneos as global regulatory pressure mounts
items.snippet: Regulators on both sides of the Atlantic are pushing for the withdrawal of the rare disease treatment that accounted for just 1% of Amgen’s 2025 revenue. Nevertheless, Amgen continues to defend the medicine, which was acquired in the $3.7 billion buyout of ChemoCentryx.

items.title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
items.snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.
